title: PHP Array Functions
content:
### PHP Array Functions

PHP has a rich set of built-in functions for array manipulation.

### Common Array Functions

| Function | Description | Example |
|----------|-------------|---------|
| `count()` | Count elements | `count($arr)` |
| `array_push()` | Add to end | `array_push($arr, 4)` |
| `array_pop()` | Remove from end | `$last = array_pop($arr)` |
| `array_shift()` | Remove from start | `$first = array_shift($arr)` |
| `array_unshift()` | Add to start | `array_unshift($arr, 0)` |
| `array_merge()` | Merge arrays | `array_merge($a, $b)` |
| `array_slice()` | Extract portion | `array_slice($arr, 1, 2)` |
| `array_splice()` | Remove/replace portion | `array_splice($arr, 1, 1, $rep)` |
| `array_reverse()` | Reverse order | `array_reverse($arr)` |
| `in_array()` | Check if value exists | `in_array("val", $arr)` |
| `array_search()` | Search for value (returns key) | `array_search("val", $arr)` |
| `array_key_exists()` | Check if key exists | `array_key_exists("key", $arr)` |

```php
<?php
$arr = [1, 2, 3];
array_push($arr, 4, 5);      // [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
$last = array_pop($arr);     // 5, arr => [1, 2, 3, 4]
$first = array_shift($arr);  // 1, arr => [2, 3, 4]
array_unshift($arr, 0);      // [0, 2, 3, 4]
?>
```

> **Key Point:** Many array functions modify the original array. Use `array_slice()` to extract without modifying the original.
codeExample:
<?php
$fruits = ["Banana", "Apple", "Cherry", "Date"];

// Merge
$more = ["Elderberry", "Fig"];
$all = array_merge($fruits, $more);
print_r($all);

// Slice (without modifying)
$slice = array_slice($fruits, 1, 2);
print_r($slice);

// Search
$index = array_search("Cherry", $fruits);
echo "Cherry is at index: $index";

// Reverse
$reversed = array_reverse($fruits);
print_r($reversed);
?>

quiz.question: Which function removes the first element of an array?
Does array_slice() modify the original array?
